---
title: "BEcon 4222 - Introduction to Programming"
subtitle: "Course Materials and Resources"
author: "Dr. Franziska Bender, Dr. Aurélien Sallin"
date: last-modified
format:
  html:
    toc: false
---

# Welcome to BEcon 4222 - Introduction to Programming

![](assets/img/banner_course.png){width=100%}

This course introduces you to programming fundamentals using Python, with applications in economics and data science.

## Course Structure

### Lectures
Access weekly lecture slides and materials through the navigation menu above.

### Exercises
Hands-on exercises to practice programming concepts.

### Administration
Course syllabus, schedule, and administrative information.

## Course Content

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-6}
### 📘 Week 1: Getting Started

**Lectures:**
- [Lecture 1a: Introduction](course_materials/week_01/01a_introduction.qmd)
- [Lecture 1b: Getting Started](course_materials/week_01/01b_gettingstarted.qmd)

**Exercises:**
- [Exercise 1: Getting Started](exercise_sessions/week_01/Ex01_gettingstarted.qmd)
- [Recap Quiz 1 (non-graded)](examination/recap_quizzes/01b_recap.qmd)

**Resources:**
- [Common Terminal Commands](course_materials/week_01/CommonTerminalCommands_BradTraversy.qmd)
:::

::: {.g-col-6}
### 📗 Week 2: Version Control with Git

**Lectures:**
- [Lecture 2a: Recap Week 1](course_materials/week_02/02_recap.qmd)
- [Lecture 2b: Version Control](course_materials/week_02/02_versioncontrol.qmd)

**Exercises:**
- [Exercise 2: First Commands in Git](exercise_sessions/week_02/Ex02_FirstCommitsInGit.qmd)

**Resources:**
- [Git Cheat Sheet (PDF)](course_materials/week_02/git-cheat-sheet-education.pdf)

**Setup:**
- Install Git ([instructions](https://git-scm.com/book/en/v2/Getting-Started-Installing-Git))
- Install VSCode extension: **Git Graph**
:::

:::

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-6}
### 📙 Week 3: Git and GitHub for Collaboration

**Lectures:**
- [Lecture 3: Version Control with Others](course_materials/week_03/03_versioncontrolwithothers.qmd)

**In-class exercise:**
- [Exercise 3: Git and GitHub for Collaboration](course_materials/week_03/ex03_git_collaboration.qmd)
- [generate_data.py](course_materials/week_03/generate_data.py)

**Exercises:**
- [Exercise 3: GitHub](exercise_sessions/week_03/Ex03_GitHub.qmd)
:::

::: {.g-col-6}
### 📒 Week 4: Introduction to Python

**Lectures:**
- [Lecture 4: Introduction to Python](course_materials/week_04/L04_IntroPython.qmd)

**Exercises:**
- [Exercise 4: Introduction to Python](exercise_sessions/week_04/Ex04_IntroPython.qmd)
:::


::: {.g-col-6}
### 📊 Week 5: Python for Data - Part I

**Lectures:**
- [Lecture 5: Pandas I](course_materials/week_05/L05_Pandas_I.qmd)
- [Lecture data: countries_data.csv](course_materials/week_05/data/countries_data.csv)

**Exercises:**
- [Exercise 5: Pandas I](exercise_sessions/week_05/Ex05_Pandas_I.qmd)
- [Exercise data: pwt_data_ex5.csv](exercise_sessions/week_05/data/pwt_data_ex5.csv)

:::

::: {.g-col-6}
### 📈 Week 6: Python for Data - Part II

**Lectures:**
- [Lecture 6: Pandas II](course_materials/week_06/L06_Pandas_II.qmd)
- [Lecture data: your_week6_file.csv](course_materials/week_06/data/unrate_data.csv)

**Exercises:**
- [Exercise 6: Pandas II](exercise_sessions/week_06/Ex06_Pandas_II.qmd)
- [Exercise data: your_ex6_file.csv](exercise_sessions/week_06/data/firm_data_ex06.csv)
:::


::: {.g-col-6}
### 📁 Week 7: Classes and Object-Oriented Programming

**Lectures:**
- [Info Group Project](course_materials/week_07/group_project.qmd)
- [Lecture 7: Classes](course_materials/week_07/L07_Classes.qmd)
- [Module example: monetary_policy.py](course_materials/week_07/monetary_policy.py)

**Exercises:**
- [Exercise 7: Classes](exercise_sessions/week_07/Ex07_Classes.qmd)
- [Exercise data: pwt_data_ex7.csv](exercise_sessions/week_07/data/pwt_data_ex7.csv)

:::



:::

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-6}
### 🗃️ Week 9: Relational Databases

**Lectures:**
- [Lecture 9: Databases & SQL](course_materials/week_09/09_databases.qmd)
- [Demo script: countries & trade flows](course_materials/week_09/demo_countries_trade.py)

**Exercises:**
- [Exercise 9: Relational Databases](exercise_sessions/week_09/E09_RelationalDB.qmd)
- [FRED data (Parquet)](exercise_sessions/week_09/fred_cache.parquet)
:::

::: {.g-col-6}
### 🗄️ Week 10: SQL Queries

**Lectures:**
- [Lecture 10: Queries](course_materials/week_10/10_queries.qmd)
- [Script: build_cepii_db.py](course_materials/week_10/build_cepii_db.py)
:::

:::

## Learning Objectives

By the end of this course, you will be able to:

- Use the terminal and command line effectively
- Write and run Python programs
- Work with data using Python libraries
- Use Git and GitHub for version control
- Apply programming concepts to economic problems
